title: Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI

modules.title: What Cloud AI Deployments Actually Do to Your Data
modules.sub: Tracing data flows and exposure risks in cloud AI pipelines
modules.description: Learners map how data moves through cloud AI inference and training pipelines, identifying where sensitive information is logged, retained, or shared with third parties. They examine the gap between vendor privacy policies and the technical reality of data handling, building a critical baseline for evaluating trust claims.

modules.title: Confidential Computing and Trusted Execution Environments Explained
modules.sub: Understanding hardware-enforced isolation as a verifiable privacy control
modules.description: Learners explore how Trusted Execution Environments use hardware attestation to protect data in use, even from cloud infrastructure operators. They evaluate what TEE-based guarantees actually prove, what they do not cover, and how to interpret attestation reports as evidence of privacy controls.

modules.title: Tool Lab: Auditing Privacy Controls in Major Cloud AI Platforms
modules.sub: Hands-on auditing of real cloud AI platform privacy configurations
modules.description: Learners use platform-specific tooling and audit interfaces across major cloud AI providers to inspect privacy configurations, attestation artifacts, and data access logs. They document their findings against a structured audit checklist, identifying gaps between advertised controls and what is technically verifiable.

modules.title: Privacy-Preserving Techniques: Federated Learning, Differential Privacy, and Homomorphic Encryption
modules.sub: Evaluating cryptographic techniques that limit or eliminate raw data exposure
modules.description: Learners compare federated learning, differential privacy, and homomorphic encryption as mechanisms that move AI processing closer to the data rather than moving data to the model. They assess the practical trade-offs of each technique — including accuracy costs and implementation complexity — and match techniques to specific organizational risk scenarios.

modules.title: Building a Verifiable Privacy Case for Regulators and Auditors
modules.sub: Translating technical privacy evidence into compliance-ready documentation
modules.description: Learners synthesize audit findings, attestation artifacts, and technical control assessments into structured documentation that satisfies regulatory frameworks such as GDPR, HIPAA, and emerging AI governance requirements. They practice presenting verifiable privacy guarantees in a format that clearly distinguishes cryptographic proof from contractual promise.

synopsis: Sending sensitive data to cloud-hosted AI models without verifiable privacy guarantees exposes organizations to data misuse, regulatory breach, and supply-chain trust failures they cannot detect until damage is done. This course teaches professionals how confidential computing, federated learning, and cryptographic privacy techniques make AI processing verifiable — not just promised — and how to audit and document those guarantees.
rationale: GDPR, HIPAA, and emerging AI-specific regulations are increasingly demanding demonstrable privacy guarantees for AI workloads, yet most security teams lack the technical vocabulary or tooling knowledge to assess or enforce them.
learning_outcome: Learners can audit a cloud AI deployment for verifiable privacy controls, identify gaps between vendor privacy claims and cryptographic guarantees, and produce compliance-ready documentation of their findings.
source_signals: Launch HN: Tinfoil (YC X25): Verifiable Privacy for Cloud AI
nearest_existing: How AI Systems Consume and Expose Personal Data
